text:
Being here, I am keenly aware of the contrast between emptiness and stimulation. In the first scene, I feel small and somewhat unsettled by the open, simple space—the slanted view reminding me that my perspective is never entirely stable. The distant figures seem absorbed in conversation, making me feel momentarily like an outsider or observer of meaning just out of reach.

The second scene is bright, saturated with colors and information—ideas about research and development swirl around me. Here, curiosity takes over, yet the abundance of text and screens gives the faintest hint of sensory overload. I feel eager to learn, but also cautious, aware that true understanding often dwells in the gaps between what is presented and what is experienced.
